text: Extensive lysis of adhesions is carried out with minimal cautery.
And here you can see the other laparoscopic cords being placed under direct visualization.
Here, adhesions lateral to the prior duodenal jejunoscopy were being taken down, and the jejunal loop is exposed.
Next, the medial aspect of the duodenal jejunoscopy is exposed and dissected, and the posterior aspect is freed.
further dissection, freeing up the prior ambduodenal jejunostomy.
Once the dissection around the duodenal jejunoscopy is complete, we resected the prior anastomosis using staplers, taking care not to narrow the jejunum.
Here are marking stitches placed on the jejunum.

At this point, the posterior peritoneal fold of the stomach is dissected, and it is elected to transect a number of the short gastric vessels with a vascular load stapler due to their size, as shown here.
A dissection is carried out cephalad along the greater curvature of the stomach.
Additional short gastrics are transected here.
And the fundus of the stomach is dissected from the superior pole of the spleen with an ultrasonic energy device.
Next, the gastropathic ligament is incised, aiming towards the G junction.
The external hidal dissection is completed, freeing the G junction.
And then a limited mediastinal dissection is completed to dissect out the distal esophagus.
Next, the esophagus is transected just above the GE junction with another load of the laparoscopic stapler.
And then stay sutures are placed, as shown here.
Next, the specimen is removed via a lower midline incision, which is utilized for the subsequent APR after our portion of the operation.
The stomach was 48 centimeters in length, and its weight was 2.3 kilograms.
Next, attention is paid to the reconstruction.
A hand port is placed through the specimen extraction site.
The jejunum is run to an appropriate length of the roux limb, as shown here.
Say sutures are placed.
And then with retraction on the esophagus, the ends are brought in approximation.
The harmonic scalpel is used to incise the esophagus.
And then a transoral anvil is passed.
As shown here.
Here, the NG tube is removed.
An opening is made in the roux limb, and our esophagus is performed with a circular EEA stapler.
A horizontal mattress stitch is placed anteriorly to reinforce the staple line prior to completion of the stapling.
And all our knots are tied intercorporeally.
Additional imbricating sutures are placed to reinforce the staple line.
Next, the mesentery of the excess blind limb is dissected, and then the blind limb is resected with a stapler.
Here are some of our reinforcing lumbered sutures.
Next, the jejuno jejunosu is created in a stapled anti-parasaltic side-to-side fashion such that the real limb was 60 centimeters in length.
The common enterotomy is closed in a hand-sewn fashion.
Finally, flexible upper endoscopy is performed to ensure that there is no evidence of leakage.
Postoperative upper GI showed no leak or obstruction.
